differentials.name: negative for c-KIT, strongly diffusely positive for desmin + smooth muscle actin
differentials.raw: negative for c-KIT, strongly diffusely positive for desmin + smooth muscle actin (DDx to GIST) @   Stomach (rare)

differentials.name: dysphagia, odynophagia, dyspepsia usually for > 2 years
differentials.raw: dysphagia, odynophagia, dyspepsia usually for > 2 years

differentials.name: hematemesis if large (rare) Site:     frequently lower + mid ⅓ of esophagus (smooth muscle portion); intramural (97%);
differentials.raw: hematemesis if large (rare) Site:     frequently lower + mid ⅓ of esophagus (smooth muscle portion); intramural (97%);

differentials.name: GIST
differentials.raw: GIST (positive for CD117 + CD34, central low attenuation, 10 x less frequent than leiomyoma)

differentials.name: Duplication cyst
differentials.raw: Duplication cyst (2nd most common benign lesion of esophagus, manifestation during childhood, may communicate with lumen)

differentials.name: Granular cell tumor
differentials.raw: Granular cell tumor (often multiple, less common)

differentials.name: Familial disease (20%): leiomyomas of uterus, vulva, tracheobronchial tree, small bowel, rectum
differentials.raw: Familial disease (20%): leiomyomas of uterus, vulva, tracheobronchial tree, small bowel, rectum

differentials.name: Alport syndrome (30%) = nephritis, high-frequency sensorineural hearing loss, congenital cataract ← germline deletion of collagen IV subunit genes on
differentials.raw: Alport syndrome (30%) = nephritis, high-frequency sensorineural hearing loss, congenital cataract ← germline deletion of collagen IV subunit genes on

differentials.name: Secondary achalasia
differentials.raw: Secondary achalasia (older individual, recent onset of dysphagia)

differentials.name: Idiopathic muscular hypertrophy of the esophagus
differentials.raw: Idiopathic muscular hypertrophy of the esophagus (in late adulthood, corkscrew appearance of esophagus with nonperistaltic contractions, cardia rarely involved)

context: N.B.:   In older literature synonymous with GIST (far more frequent, variable risk of progression and metastasis) Path:   arising from muscularis propria Histo:   low to moderate cellularity, intersecting fascicles of spindle cells with eosinophilic cytoplasm, desmin globules (occasionally) Location:   gastric cardia √  low-attenuation mass with endoluminal growth pattern √  central ulceration for tumor > 2 cm in diameter Size:   1.3-4.7 cm DDx:   GIST, schwannoma Leiomyoma of Esophagus ◊  Most common benign subepithelial tumor of esophagus; 50 x less common than esophageal carcinoma Frequency
